creator: albert.shih@obspm.fr
text:
Hi,

>I updated without issue my infra with Puppet and PuppetDB (some issues with Bolt but it is another story). So let's start with the low hanging fruits:
>
>1. After upgrading, have you restarted PuppetServer / PuppetDB / Puppet?

Well, our puppetserver/puppetdb don't run on FreeBSD, but run on a linux with the «official» (meaning puppet.com) packages (the free version).

Our infra  (a little less than 600 hosts) are mostly linux with some ( ~30-40) FreeBSD server.

So I'm only talking about puppet “agent” who run on every host.

>
>2. If you have customization in config files, you may need to sync it with updated samples (e.g. my PuppetServer config has some TLS tuning, so I had to adjust the puppetserver configuration and change some "3.0" to "3.1" in path)?
>
>3. Some gems will prevent puppetserver from starting when they are missing, for example I had to reinstall hiera-eyaml-rsa in the context of the puppetserver before it would start, so in my case:
>
># puppetserver gem install ...
>
>4. Regarding:
>> Unable to find archive module in modulepath /usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules
>This seems odd, unless you are not using a control-repo maybe?

No we don't. In fact I event don't know what are “control-repo”, our puppet code are in a git repot but we don't use r10k.

>5. If the above did not help, let's try to gather some info:
>

Here what I just do :

  pkg delete -f ruby-3.1.3_2,1 (so that uninstall everything related with ruby such as puppet7)

check with

  pkg info |grep puppet

change my /usr/local/etc/pkg/repo to activate pkg.freebsd.org and desactivate my own poudriere

Cleanup :

  rm -rf /var/puppet
  rm -rf /usr/local/etc/puppet

clean up the certificate on the puppetserver

  sudo /opt/puppetlabs/bin/puppetserver ca clean --certname fqdn_of_my_server

install the pkg

  pkg install puppet7

launch the agent

  puppet agent --server fqdn_of_my_puppetserver -vt

sign the certificate

  sudo /opt/puppetlabs/bin/puppetserver ca sign --certname fqdn_of_my_server

relaunch the agent

  puppet agent --server fqdn_of_my_puppetserver -vt

watch the 10000000 lines of sync and end up with same error

.....
Notice: /File[/var/puppet/lib/templates/RubyPwsh.cs]/ensure: defined content as '{sha256}5b8f4256b798cb623a75b3b0743f3937af83a9251b0ec9ef35d97d353697709c'
Notice: /File[/var/puppet/lib/templates/init.ps1]/ensure: defined content as '{sha256}f0b69a368c0010bc624cb12aed9c8218e6648e3dd3139f38f3be7278cc47f0f2'
Info: Loading facts
Error: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: Unable to find archive module in modulepath /usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules
Error: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/curl: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: Unable to find archive module in modulepath /usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules
Error: Could not autoload puppet/type/archive: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/curl: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: Unable to find archive module in modulepath /usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules
Error: Failed to apply catalog: Could not autoload puppet/type/archive: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/curl: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: Unable to find archive module in modulepath /usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules

The weird part is those files (just pull when run the puppet agent) seem to be here :

[root@ /var/lib]# ls -l /var/puppet/lib/puppet/provider/archive/*
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  2062 Mar 23 08:55 /var/puppet/lib/puppet/provider/archive/curl.rb
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  7027 Mar 23 08:55 /var/puppet/lib/puppet/provider/archive/ruby.rb
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  1472 Mar 23 08:55 /var/puppet/lib/puppet/provider/archive/wget.rb
[root@ /var/lib]#

I also check with other FreeBSD + puppet 7.23 + ruby 3.0 (where puppet agent work well) exactly same file (same md5sum)

About your question


># cat /usr/local/etc/puppet/puppet.conf
>[main]
>server = REDACTED
>splay = true
>[master]
>storeconfigs = true
>storeconfigs_backend = puppetdb
>reports = puppetdb
>dns_alt_names = puppet.blogreen.org
>[agent]
>usecacheonfailure = false

Before the first run of the agent I don't have puppet.conf because this file a configured by puppet since ~ 10 years, after the first *successfully* run the file is

[root@ ]# more /usr/local/etc/puppet/puppet.conf
[main]
server =
environment = production
report = true
number_of_facts_soft_limit = 2048
top_level_facts_soft_limit = 1024

But I just try your config and end up with the same error

>
># puppet config print codedir
>/usr/local/etc/puppet

Same

[root@]#  puppet config print codedir
/usr/local/etc/puppet

>
># cat /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/production/environment.conf
>modulepath = modules:site-modules
>

Don't have those directory on the agent side.

># puppet config print modulepath
>/usr/local/etc/puppet/environments/production/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/environments/production/site-modules
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
Very different                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
[root@]# puppet config print modulepath                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules:/usr/local/etc/puppet/modules                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
Thanks for everything.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
Regards
